Victorian mansion with peeling wallpaper, portraits with scratched-out eyes, doors that open by themselves.
Abandoned asylum with rusted bed frames, restraint straps hanging loose, footsteps echoing from empty wards.
Decrepit hotel lobby with flickering desk lamp, guest book filled with illegible names, elevator descending alone.
Cursed school hallway with lockers ajar, children's drawings covering walls, classroom doors locked from outside.
Fog-choked woods where tree branches form grasping hands, path disappears behind you, compass spins wildly.
Dead-tree forest under pale moon, roots erupting through cracked earth, no birds, no wind, perfect silence.
Swamp with gnarled cypress roots breaking water surface, will-o'-wisps dancing between rotting stumps, murky depths.
Silent village with empty windows watching, rocking chairs still moving, dinner tables set for absent families.
Boarded-up main street where store mannequins face outward, streetlights flicker in sequence, pharmacy door creaks open.
Abandoned carnival after midnight, carousel turning without power, prize booth dolls arranged in ritual circle.
Flooded basement with water rising slowly, shapes moving beneath surface, electrical wires sparking above waterline.
Crypt corridors lined with crumbling alcoves, stone coffins with lids pushed aside, scratch marks inside and out.
Collapsed mine shaft with support beams groaning, lantern light revealing fresh pickaxe marks, something breathing deeper in.
Sewer tunnels where pipes drip rust-red water, symbols chalked on brick walls, distant splashing growing closer.
Lighthouse on storm-battered rocks, keeper's log ending mid-sentence, beacon light rotating over churning black waves.
Isolated cabin during whiteout blizzard, windows freezing from inside, something heavy circling outside, tracks vanishing instantly.
Hilltop graveyard with tilted headstones, fresh-dug earth on century-old graves, maintenance shed door swinging in windless night.
Infinite hospital hallway where same gurney appears ahead, exit signs pointing back, fluorescent lights dying section by section.
Impossible room accessed through closet, geometry wrong, too many corners, door behind you now bricked over.
Spiral staircase descending past building's foundation, steps growing organically narrower, faint singing from bottom that never arrives.
Abandoned textile factory with looms still threaded, fabric rolls moving on motionless belts, something stitched into ceiling tarps.
Rusted freighter interior listing sideways, seawater pooling in corridors, crew quarters with meals half-eaten, no bodies found.
Decommissioned power plant at night, turbines spinning without fuel, control room dials pointing past maximum, humming growing louder.
Chapel with inverted crosses carved from inside, saints' faces melted off frescoes, communion wine turned black, altar stone cracked.
Candlelit catacombs where flames burn blue, skull arrangements spelling words in dead language, monk cells sealed with wax from inside.
